title: Section 286
content: A District Delegate shall not grant probate or letters of administration in any case in which there is contention as to the grant, or in which it otherwise appears to him that probate or letters of administration ought not to be granted in his Court. Explanation.-- "Contention" means the appearance of any one in person, or by his recognized agent, or by a pleader duly appointee to act on his behalf, to oppose the proceeding.
